Your Answer:

I agree with the previous conclusion. In addition to the classic white matter vacuolation leading to increased intracranial volume, Canavan disease often presents with progressive neurodegeneration that correlates directly with early-onset severe intellectual disability—further supporting its strong association with macrocephaly during infancy.
